Index: .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/prd.md

--- .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/prd.md	
+++ .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/prd.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,190 @@
+# PRD: ponytail-opencode-plugin
+
+## Overview
+
+Standalone OpenCode plugin fork of [ponytail](https://github.com/user/ponytail) — the "lazy senior dev mode" behavioral ruleset. Published to npm as `ponytail-opencode-plugin`. Companion plugin to `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp` for context compression.
+
+**Modeled after**: [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin) — same architecture, same hook pattern, same build pipeline.
+
+## Problem
+
+Ponytail exists as a multi-agent plugin package (10 adapters: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Copilot, Kiro, Pi, OpenCode, generic). The OpenCode adapter is a local `.mjs` file with relative imports — not npm-publishable, not installable via `opencode plugin ponytail-opencode-plugin`.
+
+Users who want ponytail in OpenCode must clone the repo and register a local path. This is friction.
+
+## Solution
+
+Extract the OpenCode adapter into a standalone npm package with:
+- TypeScript source (type safety, better DX)
+- Bun build pipeline (matching caveman-opencode-plugin)
+- Zero runtime dependencies
+- Same 3-hook pattern: `config` + `experimental.chat.system.transform` + `command.execute.before`
+- Per-session state via in-memory Map (matching caveman pattern)
+- Config via `ponytail.json` (project then global, matching caveman.json pattern)
+
+## Scope
+
+### In Scope
+- TypeScript rewrite of OpenCode adapter (`src/index.ts`)
+- Config loader (`src/config.ts`) — reads `ponytail.json`
+- State manager (`src/state.ts`) — per-session mode tracking
+- Skill/instruction generator (`src/skills/ponytail.ts`) — mode-aware instruction builder
+- Command handlers (`src/commands/mode.ts`, `src/commands/review.ts`)
+- Tests (`src/__tests__/`)
+- Build pipeline (`build.ts` + `tsconfig.json`)
+- README with install/usage docs
+- npm publishing setup (`package.json`, `files`, `keywords`)
+
+### Out of Scope
+- Multi-agent adapters (Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, etc.) — this is OpenCode-only
+- Pi extension — separate ecosystem
+- Benchmarks, examples, docs/ — not needed for plugin
+- `entire.ts` — unrelated session-tracking plugin
+- `scripts/check-rule-copies.js` — no adapter copies to check
+- AGENTS.md auto-load — plugin injects via hooks, not file
+
+## Architecture
+
+```
+ponytail-opencode-plugin/
+├── package.json          # npm package, peerDep @opencode-ai/plugin
+├── tsconfig.json         # ES2022, ESNext module
+├── build.ts              # Bun.build + tsc --emitDeclarationOnly
+├── ponytail.json.example # Example config
+├── README.md
+├── src/
+│   ├── index.ts          # Plugin entry — default export
+│   ├── config.ts         # Config loader (ponytail.json)
+│   ├── state.ts          # Per-session state (Map<sessionId, PonytailState>)
+│   ├── commands/
+│   │   ├── mode.ts       # /ponytail <level> handler
+│   │   └── review.ts     # /ponytail-review handler
+│   ├── skills/
+│   │   └── ponytail.ts   # System instruction generator (all modes)
+│   └── __tests__/
+│       ├── commands.test.ts
+│       ├── config.test.ts
+│       └── state.test.ts
+└── dist/                 # Build output (published)
+```
+
+## Hook Pattern
+
+### 1. `config` — Register commands
+```typescript
+config: async (opencodeConfig) => {
+  opencodeConfig.command ??= {}
+  opencodeConfig.command['ponytail'] = {
+    template: '',
+    description: 'Switch ponytail mode (off/lite/full/ultra)'
+  }
+  opencodeConfig.command['ponytail-review'] = {
+    template: '',
+    description: 'Run ponytail over-engineering review'
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### 2. `experimental.chat.system.transform` — Inject instructions per turn
+```typescript
+'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (input, output) => {
+  const state = getSessionState(input.sessionID)
+  if (state.mode !== 'off') {
+    output.system.push(getPonytailInstructions(state.mode))
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### 3. `command.execute.before` — Handle slash commands
+```typescript
+'command.execute.before': async (input, output) => {
+  if (input.command === 'ponytail') {
+    const level = parseLevel(input.args)
+    setSessionMode(input.sessionID, level)
+    output.parts = [{ type: 'text', text: `Ponytail mode: ${level}` }]
+  }
+}
+```
+
+## Configuration
+
+### `ponytail.json` (project root, then `~/.config/opencode/ponytail.json`)
+```json
+{
+  "enabled": true,
+  "defaultMode": "full",
+  "features": {
+    "ponytail": true,
+    "review": true
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### Modes
+| Mode    | Behavior |
+|---------|----------|
+| `off`   | No instructions injected |
+| `lite`  | Core ladder only |
+| `full`  | Full ruleset (default) |
+| `ultra` | Maximum aggression — YAGNI everything |
+| `review`| Over-engineering review mode |
+
+## Dependencies
+
+```json
+{
+  "peerDependencies": {
+    "@opencode-ai/plugin": ">=1.14.21"
+  },
+  "devDependencies": {
+    "@opencode-ai/plugin": "1.17.6",
+    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
+    "typescript": "^6.0.0"
+  }
+}
+```
+
+Zero runtime dependencies. Only Node stdlib (`fs`, `path`, `os`).
+
+## Companion Plugin
+
+README will recommend pairing with `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp`:
+```jsonc
+// opencode.jsonc
+{
+  "plugin": [
+    "@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest",
+    "ponytail-opencode-plugin@latest"
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+DCP compresses context (input tokens). Ponytail compresses code decisions (output tokens). They stack.
+
+## Publishing
+
+- **npm name**: `ponytail-opencode-plugin`
+- **License**: MIT (matching ponytail)
+- **Keywords**: `["opencode", "plugin", "ponytail", "lazy", "senior-dev", "yagni"]`
+- **Files**: `["dist"]`
+- **Build**: `bun run build.ts && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly`
+- **Pre-publish**: verify `dist/` exists, `package.json` correct
+
+## Success Criteria
+
+1. `npm publish` succeeds
+2. `opencode plugin ponytail-opencode-plugin@latest` installs and activates
+3. `/ponytail full` switches mode, instructions appear in system prompt
+4. `/ponytail off` disables injection
+5. Works alongside `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp` without conflicts
+6. Works alongside `caveman-opencode-plugin` without conflicts (different state paths)
+7. All tests pass (`bun test`)
+
+## Risks
+
+| Risk | Mitigation |
+|------|------------|
+| State path collision with original ponytail | Use `ponytail-opencode-plugin` state key, not `.ponytail-active` |
+| Dual injection if both original + fork loaded | README warns: use one or the other |
+| Skill content drift from upstream | Pin skill content to ponytail v0.1.0 SKILL.md, document source |
+| Bun runtime not available | Build output is plain ESM JS, works on Node too |

Index: .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/progress.md

--- .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/progress.md	
+++ .opencode/artifacts/ponytail-opencode-plugin/progress.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+# Progress: ponytail-opencode-plugin
+
+## Phase 1: Scaffold
+- [ ] Create `package.json` (npm metadata, peerDeps, devDeps, scripts, keywords, files)
+- [ ] Create `tsconfig.json` (ES2022, ESNext, bundler resolution)
+- [ ] Create `build.ts` (Bun.build + tsc --emitDeclarationOnly)
+- [ ] Create `ponytail.json.example`
+
+## Phase 2: Core
+- [ ] Create `src/config.ts` — config loader (project ponytail.json → global → defaults)
+- [ ] Create `src/state.ts` — per-session state Map (mode, activated timestamp)
+- [ ] Create `src/skills/ponytail.ts` — instruction generator (all modes, ladder, rules)
+- [ ] Create `src/commands/mode.ts` — /ponytail <level> handler
+- [ ] Create `src/commands/review.ts` — /ponytail-review handler
+- [ ] Create `src/index.ts` — plugin entry (3 hooks: config, system.transform, command.execute.before)
+
+## Phase 3: Tests
+- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/config.test.ts`
+- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/state.test.ts`
+- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/commands.test.ts`
+- [ ] Run `bun test` — all pass
+
+## Phase 4: Build & Verify
+- [ ] Run `bun run build.ts` — dist/ generated
+- [ ] Run `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` — .d.ts files generated
+- [ ] Verify dist/index.js exports default plugin function
+- [ ] Smoke test: register in local opencode.json, verify hooks fire
+
+## Phase 5: Publish Prep
+- [ ] Create README.md (install, usage, config, companion plugins, modes)
+- [ ] Verify `npm pack` produces correct tarball (dist/ only)
+- [ ] `npm publish --dry-run` succeeds

parts.text:
# Create: create a fork of ponytail repo:
you must create opencode plugin like this repo: https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin.git
remember: Companion Plugin
For context compression, must pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp. 

Create a specification (PRD), set up workspace, and define executable tasks — ready for `/ship`.

> **Workflow:** **`/create`** → `/ship`

## Parse Arguments

| Argument        | Default       | Description                               |
| --------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `<description>` | required      | What to build/fix (quoted string)         |

## Determine Input Type

| Input Type  | Detection            | Action                        |
| ----------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Quoted text | `"description here"` | Create PRD from description   |
| Short form  | Simple string        | Ask for more detail if needed |

## Before You Create

- **Be certain**: Only create specs you're confident have clear scope
- **Don't over-spec**: If the description is vague, ask clarifying questions first
- **Check duplicates**: Always check for existing work
- **No implementation**: This command creates specs and workspace — don't write implementation code
- **Verify PRD**: Before saving, verify all sections are filled (no placeholders)
- **Flag uncertainty**: Use `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` markers for unknowns — never guess silently

## Available Tools

| Tool      | Use When                                     |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `explore` | Finding patterns in codebase, affected files |
| `scout`   | External research, best practices            |

## Phase 1: Duplicate Check

### Memory Search

Search memory for: prior decisions, similar work.

### Existing Work Check

Check `.opencode/artifacts/.active` for existing work in progress. If active slug exists with a `spec.md`, ask user if they want to continue with `/ship` instead.

## Phase 3: Choose Research Depth

Ask user before spawning agents:

```typescript
question({
  questions: [
    {
      header: "Research Depth",
      question: "How much codebase research do you need?",
      options: [
        {
          label: "Deep (Recommended for complex work)",
          description: "3-5 agents: patterns, tests, deps, best practices (~2 min)",
        },
        {
          label: "Standard",
          description: "2 agents: patterns + tests (~1 min)",
        },
        {
          label: "Minimal",
          description: "1 agent: quick file scan (~30 sec)",
        },
        {
          label: "Skip",
          description: "I know the codebase, use existing knowledge",
        },
      ],
    },
  ],
});
```

## Phase 4: Gather Context

Based on research depth choice, spawn agents:

**If Deep:**

- 3x `explore` (patterns, tests, deps)
- 1x `scout` (feature/epic)
- 1x `review` (epic)

**If Standard:**

- 2x `explore` (patterns, tests)
- 1x `scout` (feature/epic only)

**If Minimal:**

- 1x `explore` (patterns)

**If Skip:**

- No agents, use existing AGENTS.md context

**While agents run**, ask clarifying questions if the description lacks scope or expected outcome. For bugs, also ask for reproduction steps and expected vs actual behavior.

## Phase 5: Initialize Plan

Extract title and description from `create a fork of ponytail repo:
you must create opencode plugin like this repo: https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin.git
remember: Companion Plugin
For context compression, must pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp. `:

- If user provided a single line, use it for both title and description.
- If user provided multiple lines, use first line as title and full text as description.

Derive a kebab-case slug from the title. This slug becomes the feature's namespace:

```bash
SLUG=$(echo "$TITLE" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed 's/[^a-z0-9 ]//g' | tr ' ' '-' | sed 's/--*/-/g; s/^-//; s/-$//')
mkdir -p ".opencode/artifacts/$SLUG"
echo "$SLUG" > ".opencode/artifacts/.active"
```

## Phase 6: Determine PRD Rigor

Not every change needs a full spec. Assess complexity to choose the right PRD level:

| Signal | Lite PRD | Full PRD |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Scope | Simple, single-concern | Cross-cutting, multi-system |
| Files affected | 1-3 | 4+ |
| Research depth | Skip or Minimal | Standard or Deep |
| Description | "Fix X in Y" | "Implement X with Y and Z" |

**Auto-detect:** If research was Skip/Minimal AND description is a single sentence → default to Lite.

### Lite PRD Format

For simple, well-scoped work (bugs, small tasks):

```markdown
# [Title]

## Problem
[1-2 sentences: what's wrong or what's needed]

## Solution
[1-2 sentences: what to do]

## Affected Files
- `src/path/to/file.ts`

## Tasks
- [ ] [Task description] → Verify: `[command]`

## Success Criteria
- Verify: `npm run typecheck && npm run lint`
- Verify: `[specific test or check]`
```

### Full PRD Format

For features and complex work, use the full template:

Read the PRD template and write it to the active feature's spec (`.opencode/artifacts/$(cat .opencode/artifacts/.active)/spec.md`).

## Phase 7: Write PRD

Copy and fill the PRD template (lite or full) using context from Phase 4.

**If Lite PRD:** Fill the lite format directly. No template file needed.

**If Full PRD:** Read the template and fill all required sections:

| Section           | Source                                                     | Required          |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Problem Statement | User description + clarifying questions                    | Always            |
| Scope (In/Out)    | User input + codebase exploration                          | Always            |
| Proposed Solution | Codebase patterns + user intent                            | Always            |
| Success Criteria  | User verification + test commands (must include `Verify:`) | Always            |
| Technical Context | Explore agent findings                                     | Always            |
| Affected Files    | Explore agent findings (real paths from Phase 4)           | Always            |
| Tasks             | Derived from scope + solution                              | Always            |
| Risks             | Codebase exploration                                       | Feature/epic only |
| Open Questions    | Unresolved items from Phase 4                              | If any exist      |

### Task Format

Tasks must follow this format:

- Title with `[category]` tag
- One-sentence **end state** description (not step-by-step)
- Metadata block: `depends_on`, `parallel`, `conflicts_with`, `files`
- At least one verification command per task

## Phase 8: Validate PRD

Before saving, verify:

- [ ] No placeholder text remains (e.g., "[Clear description", "[List what's allowed]")
- [ ] Success criteria include `Verify:` commands
- [ ] Technical context references actual `src/` paths from exploration
- [ ] Affected files list real paths
- [ ] Tasks have `[category]` headings
- [ ] Each task has verification
- [ ] No implementation code in the PRD
- [ ] No unresolved `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` markers remain (convert to Open Questions or resolve)

If any check fails, fix it — don't ask the user.

## Phase 9: Prepare Workspace

### Workspace Check

```bash
git status --porcelain
git branch --show-current
```

- If uncommitted changes: ask user to stash, commit, or continue

### Create Branch

### Workspace Setup

Set up the workspace: create branch, install deps if needed.

Additionally offer a "Create worktree" option:

```typescript
skill({ name: "using-git-worktrees" });
```

## Phase 10: Convert PRD to Tasks

Convert PRD markdown → executable JSON (`prd.json`).

## Phase 11: Report

Output:

1. Summary: task count, success criteria count, affected files count
2. Branch name and workspace (if claimed)
3. Active feature: `.opencode/artifacts/$(cat .opencode/artifacts/.active)/`
4. Next step: `/ship` (or `/plan` for complex work)

---

## Related Commands

| Need               | Command      |
| ------------------ | ------------ |
| Research first     | `/research`  |
| Plan after spec    | `/plan`      |
| Implement and ship | `/ship`      |

Let me analyze this task carefully.

The user wants me to:
1. Create a fork of the ponytail repo
2. Create an opencode plugin similar to https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin.git
3. It's a companion plugin for context compression, must pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp
4. Create a specification (PRD), set up workspace, and define executable tasks
5. Ready for `/ship`

This is a `/create` workflow - I need to:
1. Check for duplicates
2. Research the reference repo
3. Gather context
4. Create PRD
5. Set up workspace
6. Define tasks

Let me start by:
1. Searching memory for prior decisions
2. Checking existing work
3. Researching the reference repo (caveman-opencode-plugin)
4. Understanding the ponytail repo structure

Let me do the duplicate check and research in parallel.

input.prompt:
Explore the ponytail repo at /home/linhlb/work/ponytail for:

1. **Project structure**: What's the directory layout? What language/framework? Package manager?
2. **Existing plugin patterns**: Are there any existing opencode plugins? Check `.opencode/plugins/`, `package.json`, any plugin-related files.
3. **Build system**: How is the project built? TypeScript config? Build scripts?
4. **Configuration files**: Check `opencode.json`, `opencode.jsonc`, `.opencode/` directory structure.
5. **AGENTS.md and project conventions**: What coding standards exist?
6. **Existing dependencies**: What's in package.json? Any opencode-related deps already?

Return: complete directory tree (depth 3), key config file contents, existing plugin code if any, package.json contents, and any opencode-related configuration.

Here's the full picture.

---

## 1. Project Structure — Directory Tree (depth 3)

**Language:** JavaScript (Node.js CommonJS for hooks, ESM for OpenCode plugin + pi-extension).  
**Package managers:** npm (root `package.json` minimal, `.opencode/package.json` has the real deps).  
**No TypeScript build step** — `.ts` file (`entire.ts`) is an auto-generated plugin, not compiled. Everything else is plain JS/MJS/Markdown.

---

## 2. Existing OpenCode Plugins (`.opencode/plugins/`)

Two plugins in `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/.opencode/plugins/`:

### `ponytail.mjs` — the real one
**Path:** `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`  
**Registered in:** `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/opencode.json` line 3: `"plugin": ["./.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs"]`

Pattern:
```js
export default async ({ client } = {}) => {
  return {
    'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (_input, output) => {
      // Reads mode from ~/.config/opencode/.ponytail-active
      // Appends ponytail instructions to output.system[]
    },
    'command.execute.before': async (input) => {
      // Catches /ponytail <level> commands, writes flag file
    },
  };
};
```

It reuses the shared `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js` and `hooks/ponytail-config.js` (bridged via `createRequire`).

### `entire.ts` — auto-generated, do not edit
**Path:** `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/.opencode/plugins/entire.ts`  
Auto-generated by `entire enable --agent opencode`. Hooks into session lifecycle events (`session.created`, `message.updated`, `session.status`, `session.deleted`, etc.) and pipes them to the Entire CLI. Not ponytail-specific.

---

## 3. Build System

**No build step.** The project is pure JavaScript/Markdown.

- No `tsconfig.json`, no `tsc`, no bundler.
- No `Makefile`, no build scripts.
- Tests use Node's built-in test runner (`node --test`).
- Script check: `scripts/check-rule-copies.js` — runs as a CI check to verify host-specific rule copies haven't drifted from `AGENTS.md`. Invariant: run `node scripts/check-rule-copies.js`.

**Test commands:**
- `node --test tests/hooks.test.js` (hooks)
- `node --test tests/opencode-plugin.test.js` (OpenCode plugin)
- `node --test pi-extension/test/*.test.js` (pi extension)

---

## 4. Configuration Files

### `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/opencode.json`
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "plugin": ["./.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs"]
}
```
Single plugin entry. Schema points to opencode.ai config schema.

### `.opencode/` directory
| Path                                 | Purpose                                    |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ |
| `.opencode/package.json`               | Depends on `@opencode-ai/plugin` v1.17.6     |
| `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`       | The ponytail OpenCode plugin               |
| `.opencode/plugins/entire.ts`          | Auto-generated Entire CLI plugin           |
| `.opencode/command/ponytail.md`        | `/ponytail` slash command (switch intensity) |
| `.opencode/command/ponytail-review.md` | `/ponytail-review` slash command             |

### Ponytail config (not in repo, per-user)
Resolution order for default mode:
1. `PONYTAIL_DEFAULT_MODE` env var
2. `~/.config/ponytail/config.json` → `{ "defaultMode": "lite" }`
3. Hard default: `'full'`

Modes: `off`, `lite`, `full`, `ultra`, `review`.

---

## 5. AGENTS.md and Project Conventions

**Path:** `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/AGENTS.md` — 25 lines, the "lazy senior dev mode" contract.

Key conventions:
- **The Ladder:** YAGNI → stdlib → native platform → existing dep → one-liner → minimum code
- **No unrequested abstractions**, no avoidable deps, no boilerplate
- **Deletion over addition**, boring over clever, fewest files
- **Mark simplifications** with `ponytail:` comment (naming ceiling + upgrade path)
- **Non-trivial logic** leaves ONE runnable check (assert/self-check or small test, no frameworks)
- Five host-specific copies must stay in sync (checked by `scripts/check-rule-copies.js`):
  - `.cursor/rules/ponytail.mdc`
  - `.windsurf/rules/ponytail.md`
  - `.clinerules/ponytail.md`
  - `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
  - `.kiro/steering/ponytail.md`

---

## 6. Existing Dependencies

### Root `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/package.json`
```json
{
  "name": "ponytail",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "pi": {
    "extensions": ["./pi-extension/index.js"],
    "skills": ["./skills"]
  }
}
```
**Zero runtime dependencies** at root level. This is the pi-package descriptor.

### `.opencode/package.json`
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": "1.17.6"
  }
}
```
Only one dependency: `@opencode-ai/plugin` — the OpenCode plugin SDK (used by `entire.ts` for the `Plugin` type import, but NOT used by `ponytail.mjs` — the ponytail plugin is dependency-free).

### `pi-extension/package.json`
Private dev package, no external deps. Just uses Node built-in `node:module`.

---

## Architecture Summary

```
AGENTS.md  ←─────────── compact contract (copied to 5 host-specific files)
    │
    ▼
skills/ponytail/SKILL.md  ←── canonical, full ruleset (intensity table, ladder, examples)
    │
    ▼
hooks/ponytail-instructions.js  ←── shared instruction builder (reads SKILL.md, filters by mode)
hooks/ponytail-config.js        ←── shared config resolver (env > config.json > 'full')
    │
    ├── hooks/ponytail-activate.js     (Claude Code SessionStart hook)
    ├── hooks/ponytail-mode-tracker.js (Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook)
    ├── .opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs (OpenCode plugin — system.transform + command.execute.before)
    └── pi-extension/index.js          (Pi extension — registerCommand + before_agent_start)
```

The shared `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js` and `hooks/ponytail-config.js` are the single source of truth imported by all three host adapters (Claude Code, OpenCode, Pi). Adding a new host means importing those two modules and wiring host-specific hooks — same pattern as `ponytail.mjs`.
</task_result>
</task>

state.output:
<task id="ses_139a666d6ffePTHZqgOJQNiL2O" state="completed">
<task_result>
Complete findings compiled.

---

## OpenCode Plugin Architecture — Full Research

### 1. Plugin Type System

**SDK package**: `@opencode-ai/plugin` v1.15.13  
**Location**: `/home/linhlb/.config/opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/`

The canonical `Plugin` type — `/home/linhlb/.config/opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts:51`:
```ts
export type Plugin = (input: PluginInput, options?: PluginOptions) => Promise<Hooks>;
```

`PluginInput` (line 36-46):
```ts
export type PluginInput = {
    client: ReturnType<typeof createOpencodeClient>;
    project: Project;
    directory: string;       // working directory
    worktree: string;
    experimental_workspace: { register(type: string, adapter: WorkspaceAdapter): void };
    serverUrl: URL;
    $: BunShell;             // Bun shell for running commands
};
```

The `Hooks` interface (lines 173-317) defines all hook points a plugin can implement:

| Hook                                 | Purpose                                                              |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `event`                                | Receive all OpenCode events (session.created, message.updated, etc.) |
| `config`                               | Modify OpenCode config at startup (add commands, etc.)               |
| `tool`                                 | Register custom tools                                                |
| `auth`                                 | Custom auth providers                                                |
| `provider`                             | Custom model providers                                               |
| `chat.message`                         | Intercept new messages                                               |
| `chat.params`                          | Modify LLM parameters before send                                    |
| `chat.headers`                         | Modify HTTP headers before send                                      |
| `permission.ask`                       | Override permission decisions                                        |
| `command.execute.before`               | Intercept slash commands                                             |
| `tool.execute.before`                  | Intercept tool calls                                                 |
| `tool.execute.after`                   | Post-process tool results                                            |
| `shell.env`                            | Inject environment variables for shell commands                      |
| `experimental.chat.system.transform`   | Append to system prompt every turn                                   |
| `experimental.chat.messages.transform` | Transform full message history                                       |
| `experimental.session.compacting`      | Customize compaction prompt                                          |
| `experimental.compaction.autocontinue` | Control auto-continue after compaction                               |
| `tool.definition`                      | Modify tool definitions                                              |
| `experimental.text.complete`           | Custom text completion                                               |
| `dispose`                              | Cleanup on plugin unload                                             |

---

### 2. Plugin Registration Patterns

**Config file**: `/home/linhlb/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc` (lines 155-163):
```jsonc
"plugin": [
    "@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest",
    "@franlol/opencode-md-table-formatter@latest",
    "openslimedit@latest",
    "opencodex-fast@latest",
    "opencode-snippets@latest",
    "opencode-bytheway@latest",
    "@morphllm/opencode-morph-plugin@latest",
    "caveman-opencode-plugin@latest"
]
```

The `Config` type (line 48) also supports options:
```ts
export type Config = Omit<SDKConfig, "plugin"> & {
    plugin?: Array<string | [string, PluginOptions]>;
};
```

---

### 3. Three Plugin Deployment Architectures

#### A. NPM-Published Plugins (dominant pattern)

Installed via `opencode plugin <name>@latest` or manual `bun i` into `~/.config/opencode/node_modules/`.

**Structure (morph-plugin — `/home/linhlb/.config/opencode/node_modules/@morphllm/opencode-morph-plugin/`)**:
- `package.json` declares `@opencode-ai/plugin` as dependency
- `dist/index.js` exports default async function returning Hooks
- Registers tools via `tool` hook, uses event hooks for compaction
- Ships with `instructions/` directory for model tool-selection policy

**Structure (caveman-opencode-plugin — `/home/linhlb/.cache/opencode/packages/caveman-opencode-plugin@latest/node_modules/caveman-opencode-plugin/`)**:
- `package.json` has `"type": "module"`
- `dist/index.js` exports default plugin function
- Uses `config` hook to register commands, `experimental.chat.system.transform` to inject system prompt, `command.execute.before` for slash commands
- Reads config from `caveman.json` (project-dir first, then `~/.config/opencode/`)

#### B. Local Repo Plugins (`.opencode/plugins/*.ts` or `.mjs`)

**ponytail.mjs** — `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`:
```js
import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
export default async ({ client } = {}) => {
    return {
        'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (_input, output) => {
            output.system.push(getPonytailInstructions(mode));
        },
        'command.execute.before': async (input) => {
            // handle /ponytail <level>
        },
    };
};
```
- Registered via relative path: `"plugin": ["./.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs"]`
- Imports from shared hooks (`../../hooks/ponytail-instructions`, `../../hooks/ponytail-config`)
- Persists mode to `~/.config/opencode/.ponytail-active` flag file

**entire.ts** — `/home/linhlb/work/ponytail/.opencode/plugins/entire.ts`:
```ts
import type { Plugin } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"
export const EntirePlugin: Plugin = async ({ directory }) => {
    return {
        event: async ({ event }) => {
            // Handle session.created, message.updated, session.compacted, etc.
            // Spawns `entire hooks opencode <hookName>` shell commands
        }
    }
}
```
- Uses the `event` hook (lower-level, raw event stream)
- Tracks sessions, messages, models in memory
- Forwards structured JSON to external CLI tool via `Bun.spawn`/`Bun.spawnSync`

#### C. Legacy Config-Dir Plugins (`~/.config/opencode/plugins/`)

**caveman** — `/home/linhlb/.config/opencode/plugins/caveman/`:
- `package.json` — `"type": "module"`, `"main": "plugin.js"`, `"private": true`
- `plugin.js` — uses `session.created` and `tui.prompt.append` hooks (older API)
- `caveman-config.cjs` — shared helpers loaded via `createRequire`
- Persists mode to `~/.config/opencode/.caveman-active` flag file
- Parses natural-language activation ("talk like caveman") and slash commands (`/caveman`)

---

### 4. Plugin Caching

NPM-published plugins cached at:  
**`~/.cache/opencode/packages/<name>/`**

Example: `/home/linhlb/.cache/opencode/packages/@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest/`
- Contains `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `node_modules/` tree
- `package.json` has the pinned version (e.g., `"@tarquinen/opencode-dcp": "3.1.12"`)

---

### 5. Key Plugin Examples Analyzed

| Plugin                    | Type         | Hooks Used                                                                                             | Key Pattern                                          |
| ------------------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| **ponytail.mjs**              | Local `.mjs`   | `experimental.chat.system.transform`, `command.execute.before`                                             | System prompt injection + slash-command persistence  |
| **entire.ts**                 | Local `.ts`    | `event` (session.created, message.updated, session.compacted, session.deleted, server.instance.disposed) | Event-driven, spawns external CLI                    |
| **caveman-opencode-plugin**   | NPM (0.1.4)  | `config`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`, `command.execute.before`                                     | Config registration + system prompt + slash commands |
| **opencode-dcp** (@tarquinen) | NPM (3.1.12) | `tool`, `experimental.chat.messages.transform`, event hooks                                                | Custom compress tool, message history transformation |
| **morph-plugin**              | NPM (2.0.13) | `tool` (3 tools), event hooks, `experimental.session.compacting`                                           | Tool registration + compaction integration           |
| **opencodex-fast**            | NPM          | `chat.headers` (intercepts HTTP to inject service tier)                                                  | Modifies provider requests                           |
| **caveman (legacy)**          | Config-dir   | `session.created`, `tui.prompt.append`                                                                     | Older hook API, flag-file persistence                |

---

### 6. Plugin Discovery / Resolution

1. OpenCode reads `plugin` array from `opencode.jsonc`
2. For each entry: tries `~/.config/opencode/node_modules/<name>/` first
3. Falls back to `node_modules/<name>/` in project
4. Falls back to npm registry for `@latest`-tagged entries (cached to `~/.cache/opencode/packages/`)
5. For relative paths (e.g., `./.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`): resolved relative to project root
6. Each plugin's default export is called with `PluginInput`, returning `Hooks`
7. All plugins' hooks are merged — multiple plugins can register the same hook, all fire

---

### 7. Important Constraints

- **Bun runtime required** for plugins that load ESM (all official plugins use Bun via `Bun.spawn`, `Blob`, `TextEncoder`)
- Plugin failures **must not crash OpenCode** — all examples wrap in try/catch with silent ignore
- Sync vs async hooks matter: `turn-end`/`session-end` use sync spawn so they complete before process exit
- State persistence: flag files at `~/.config/opencode/` pattern (`*.jsonc`, `.*-active`)
</task_result>
</task>

state.output:
<task id="ses_139a63fcaffe116uWiEy7yF5mB" state="completed">
<task_result>
Complete analysis. Here are the findings.

---

# OpenCode Plugin Ecosystem Analysis

## 1. caveman-opencode-plugin — Complete Breakdown

### What it is
A caveman communication mode plugin for OpenCode. Adapts [JuliusBrussee/caveman](https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/caveman) (54k+ stars) behavioral rules into an OpenCode hook-based plugin. Cuts ~75% output tokens by making AI respond in terse fragments.

**Source:** [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin) — 71 stars, 9 commits, 6 npm releases (v0.1.0 → v0.1.5)

### Repository Structure
```
src/
├── index.ts          # Plugin entry — exports cavemanPlugin
├── config.ts         # Config loader (caveman.json)
├── state.ts          # Per-session mode state (Map<sessionId, CavemanState>)
├── setup.ts          # Interactive setup wizard
├── commands/
│   ├── commit.ts     # /caveman-commit handler
│   ├── mode.ts       # /caveman (/caveman-mode) handler
│   └── review.ts     # /caveman-review handler
├── skills/
│   ├── caveman.ts    # System instruction generator (all modes + examples)
│   ├── commit.ts     # Commit message instruction
│   └── review.ts     # Code review instruction
└── __tests__/
    ├── commands.test.ts
    ├── config.test.ts
    └── state.test.ts
build.ts               # Bun build script
setup.sh               # Bash setup wizard
caveman.json.example   # Example config
```

**Source:** `src/` directory at [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/tree/main/src](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/tree/main/src)

### Plugin API — How it registers

**Entry point** (`src/index.ts`):
```typescript
import type { Plugin, Hooks } from '@opencode-ai/plugin'

const cavemanPlugin: Plugin = async () => {
  const hooks: Hooks = {
    // Hook implementations
  }
  return hooks
}

export default cavemanPlugin
```

**Source:** [src/index.ts:1-3](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/index.ts)

### Hooks Used (3 hooks)

| Hook                               | Purpose                                                                                                                              |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `config`                             | Registers slash commands (`/caveman`, `/caveman-mode`, `/caveman-commit`, `/caveman-review`) in opencode config                              |
| `experimental.chat.system.transform` | Injects caveman system instruction into **every** LLM call. Reads mode from `state.ts` per sessionID. Appends to `output.system[]` array     |
| `command.execute.before`             | Intercepts commands before model sees them. Handles `/caveman <mode>`, `/caveman-commit`, `/caveman-review`. Mutates `output.parts` directly |

**Source:** [src/index.ts:10-80](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/index.ts)

### Dependencies
- **peerDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin >=1.14.21`
- **devDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin@1.14.21`, `@types/node`, `typescript@6.0.3`
- **Zero runtime dependencies** — only imports `fs`, `path`, `os`, `process` (Node stdlib)

**Source:** [package.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/package.json)

### Build/Publish
- **Build:** `bun run build.ts` (Bun.build API) + `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly`
- **Output:** ESM to `dist/` directory
- **TypeScript config:** target ES2022, module ESNext, bundler resolution
- **Published files:** `dist/` only
- **No CI/CD workflow** visible (no `.github/workflows/`)

**Source:** [build.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/build.ts), [tsconfig.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/tsconfig.json)

### Relationship to DCP
Mentioned in README as **companion plugin**: "For context compression, pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp." No code dependency. They solve different problems — caveman reduces **output** tokens, DCP reduces **input/context** tokens.

### Configuration
Two config sources, checked in order:
1. `./caveman.json` (project root)
2. `~/.config/opencode/caveman.json` (global)

Schema:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "defaultMode": "full",
  "features": {
    "caveman": true,
    "commit": true,
    "review": true
  }
}
```

**Source:** [src/config.ts:1-48](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/src/config.ts), [caveman.json.example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin/main/caveman.json.example)

---

## 2. @tarquinen/opencode-dcp — Dynamic Context Pruning

### What it is
AGPL-3.0 plugin that optimizes token usage by pruning obsolete tool outputs from conversation context. 3.3k stars, 1,349 commits, v3.1.12. 10.5k weekly downloads. The most popular OpenCode plugin.

**Source:** [Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning), [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@tarquinen/opencode-dcp)

### How context compression works
Uses OpenCode's `experimental.chat.messages.transform` hook to modify messages **before** they reach the LLM. Session history is never modified — pruned content replaced with placeholders. Three mechanisms:

1. **Compress tool** — LLM-callable tool that replaces stale conversation spans with technical summaries
   - `range` mode: compress contiguous spans into summary blocks
   - `message` mode (experimental): compress individual messages
   - Nested summaries: overlapping compressions preserve info through layers
2. **Automatic cleanup** — deduplication (remove duplicate tool outputs), error purging
3. **AI nudges** — reminds model to use compress after N tool results

**Source:** [DCP index.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning/master/index.ts)

### Hooks Used (5 hooks)

| Hook                                 | Purpose                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `experimental.chat.system.transform`   | Inject DCP system prompt instructions                                            |
| `experimental.chat.messages.transform` | Main hook — prune, deduplicate, inject nudges, sync compression blocks           |
| `experimental.text.complete`           | Strip hallucinations from completed text                                         |
| `command.execute.before`               | Handle `/dcp` subcommands (stats, sweep, manual, compress, decompress, recompress) |
| `event`                                | Track compression timing via `message.part.updated` events                         |
| `config`                               | Register `dcp` command, add `compress` to primary_tools, sync permissions            |
| `tool`                                 | Register `compress` as a custom tool the model can call                            |

### Dependencies
- **peerDependencies:** `@opencode-ai/plugin >=1.4.3`
- **runtime deps:** `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer`, `@opencode-ai/sdk`, `jsonc-parser`
- **devDeps:** tsup, tsx, prettier, typescript

### Build
- **Build:** `tsup` + `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` (more mature than caveman's Bun-only approach)
- **Pre-publish check:** `scripts/verify-package.mjs`

---

## 3. OpenCode Plugin API — Complete Reference

### Plugin Architecture
A plugin is a TypeScript/JS module that exports a default function returning a `Hooks` object. The function receives a context object.

**Source:** [opencode.ai/docs/plugins/](https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/)

### Context Object
```typescript
{ project, client, $, directory, worktree }
```
- `project` — Current project info
- `client` — OpenCode SDK client for AI interaction
- `$` — Bun shell API for executing commands
- `directory` — CWD
- `worktree` — Git worktree path

### Available Hooks

**Command Events:** `command.execute.before`, `command.executed`

**File Events:** `file.edited`, `file.watcher.updated`

**Message Events:** `message.part.removed`, `message.part.updated`, `message.removed`, `message.updated`

**Session Events:** `session.created`, `session.compacted`, `session.deleted`, `session.diff`, `session.error`, `session.idle`, `session.status`, `session.updated`

**Tool Events:** `tool.execute.before`, `tool.execute.after`

**Experimental Chat Hooks:**
- `experimental.chat.system.transform` — Modify system prompt array per LLM call. Input: `{ sessionID?, model }`, Output: `{ system: string[] }`
- `experimental.chat.messages.transform` — Modify message array before LLM sees it. Input: `{}`, Output: `{ messages: { info: Message, parts: Part[] }[] }`
- `experimental.text.complete` — Transform completed text output
- `experimental.session.compacting` — Customize compaction context

**Other:** `event` (generic event handler), `config` (modify opencode config), `tool` (register custom tools), `shell.env` (inject env vars), `tui.*` hooks

### Custom Tools
```typescript
import { type Plugin, tool } from "@opencode-ai/plugin"

export const MyPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
  return {
    tool: {
      mytool: tool({
        description: "Description",
        args: { foo: tool.schema.string() },
        async execute(args, context) {
          return `Result: ${args.foo}`
        },
      }),
    },
  }
}
```

### Plugin Loading
1. Global config → Project config
2. npm packages installed via Bun at startup, cached in `~/.cache/opencode/node_modules/`
3. Local files: `.opencode/plugins/` (project), `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` (global)

---

## 4. Other OpenCode Plugin Examples

| Plugin                       | Author        | What it does                                                                                                                            | Stars/Downloads      |
| ---------------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp`      | tarquinen     | Dynamic context pruning                                                                                                                 | 3.3k stars, 10.5k/wk |
| `caveman-opencode-plugin`      | dantesCode    | Caveman communication mode                                                                                                              | 71 stars             |
| `@mumme-it/opencode-caveman`   | Mumme-IT      | Alternative caveman — uses `experimental.chat.messages.transform`, `experimental.session.compacting`, custom `caveman_set_level` tool, skills | 0 stars              |
| `opencode-caveman`             | dxvapor       | Minimal caveman — no config, just install                                                                                               | 200/wk               |
| `opencode-sessions`            | malhashemi    | Session management UI                                                                                                                   | —                    |
| `opencode-smart-title`         | tarquinen     | Auto-generate session titles                                                                                                            | 159/wk               |
| `opencode-vim`                 | tarquinen     | Vim-style editing                                                                                                                       | 269/wk               |
| `oc-tps`                       | tarquinen     | Tokens-per-second display                                                                                                               | 161/wk               |
| `opencode-helicone-session`    | —             | Observability/tracing                                                                                                                   | —                    |
| `opencode-wakatime`            | angristan     | Time tracking                                                                                                                           | —                    |
| `opencode-magi`                | —             | Multi-agent PR review                                                                                                                   | 17k/wk               |
| `opencode-subagent-statusline` | joaquinvesapa | Subagent status in statusline                                                                                                           | 28k/wk               |
| `@braintrust/trace-opencode`   | braintrust    | LLM tracing to Braintrust                                                                                                               | 88k/wk               |

---

## 5. How to Create a Similar Plugin — Recipe

### Minimal plugin file structure
```
my-plugin/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── build.ts              # Bun.build or tsup
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Default export: Plugin function
│   ├── config.ts         # Optional config loader
│   ├── state.ts          # Optional runtime state
│   ├── commands/         # Command handlers
│   ├── skills/           # Prompt generators / system instructions
│   └── __tests__/
└── README.md
```

### package.json template
```json
{
  "name": "my-opencode-plugin",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "bun run build.ts && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
    "dev": "opencode plugin dev"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": ">=1.14.21"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": "1.14.21",
    "typescript": "^6.0.0"
  },
  "keywords": ["opencode", "plugin"],
  "license": "MIT"
}
```

### Core plugin pattern
```typescript
import type { Plugin, Hooks } from '@opencode-ai/plugin'

const myPlugin: Plugin = async (ctx) => {
  const hooks: Hooks = {
    // 1. Register commands
    config: async (opencodeConfig) => {
      opencodeConfig.command ??= {}
      opencodeConfig.command['mycommand'] = {
        template: '',
        description: 'My custom command'
      }
    },

    // 2. Inject system instructions every turn
    'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (input, output) => {
      output.system.push('My custom instruction for every LLM call')
    },

    // 3. Intercept commands before model sees them
    'command.execute.before': async (input, output) => {
      if (input.command === 'mycommand') {
        output.parts = [/* custom response parts */]
      }
    },

    // 4. Register custom tools the model can call
    tool: {
      mytool: {
        description: 'My custom tool',
        args: { param: { type: 'string' } },
        async execute(args, context) {
          return `Tool result for ${args.param}`
        }
      }
    }
  }

  return hooks
}

export default myPlugin
```

### Key patterns from the ecosystem

1. **Config registration** — Use `config` hook to register commands, skills paths, tools
2. **System prompt injection** — Use `experimental.chat.system.transform` to inject per-turn system instructions (like caveman rules)
3. **Message transformation** — Use `experimental.chat.messages.transform` to modify/reduce message context (like DCP pruning)
4. **Command interception** — Use `command.execute.before` to handle commands without LLM round-trips. Mutate `output.parts` directly
5. **Compaction survival** — Use `experimental.session.compacting` to ensure plugin state persists through `/compact`
6. **Per-session state** — Use `Map<sessionID, State>` (caveman pattern) or file-based persistence (DCP pattern via `~/.config/opencode/`)
7. **Config discovery** — Check project then global config, use sensible defaults (both plugins follow this)

### Key differences between plugins

| Aspect         | caveman-opencode-plugin      | @tarquinen/opencode-dcp                |
| -------------- | ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Complexity     | Simple (3 hooks, ~200 lines) | Complex (5+ hooks, thousands of lines) |
| Dependencies   | 0 runtime deps               | 3 runtime deps                         |
| Build          | Bun.build                    | tsup                                   |
| Config         | caveman.json                 | dcp config in opencode.json            |
| State          | In-memory Map                | File-persisted + session sync          |
| License        | MIT                          | AGPL-3.0                               |
| Test framework | bun test                     | Node built-in test runner + tsx        |
</task_result>
</task>

input.prompt:
Review the ponytail repo at /home/linhlb/work/ponytail to assess:

1. **Is this repo already an opencode plugin?** Check if ponytail itself is a plugin or a project that uses plugins.
2. **What would "forking" mean here?** The user wants to "create a fork of ponytail repo" as an opencode plugin. Assess whether ponytail is already structured as a plugin or needs restructuring.
3. **Existing opencode integration**: Check all opencode-related files:
   - `.opencode/` directory contents
   - `opencode.json` or `opencode.jsonc`
   - Any plugin registrations
   - AGENTS.md references to opencode
4. **What's the relationship between ponytail and caveman?** Both seem to be opencode customization layers. How do they relate?
5. **Risks**: What could go wrong creating a plugin fork?

Return: assessment of current state, what needs to change, and risks.

state.output:
<task id="ses_139a62db1ffe5usi2Aa0O2r78w" state="completed">
<task_result>
## Assessment

### 1. Is ponytail already an opencode plugin?

**Yes. Ponytail IS already a multi-agent plugin package**, not just a project that uses plugins. It ships adapters for **10 agents**: Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Copilot, Aider, Kiro, and generic agents.

The OpenCode plugin specifically lives at:
- `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs` — the server plugin (ES module, uses `experimental.chat.system.transform` + `command.execute.before`)
- `.opencode/command/ponytail.md` + `ponytail-review.md` — slash commands
- `.opencode/package.json` — depends on `@opencode-ai/plugin@1.17.6`
- `opencode.json` — registers the plugin: `{ "plugin": ["./.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs"] }`

### 2. What "forking" means here

Ponytail is **already structured as a plugin**. A fork is just a git fork + rename. No restructuring needed. The architecture is:

```
skills/           ← source of truth (SKILL.md files)
hooks/            ← shared instruction builder (CommonJS)
.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs  ← thin OpenCode adapter, imports hooks/
.claude-plugin/   ← thin Claude Code adapter
.codex-plugin/    ← thin Codex adapter
.cursor/rules/    ← static rule copy
.windsurf/rules/  ← static rule copy
...etc
```

The OpenCode adapter (`.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs:17-18`) does `require('../../hooks/ponytail-instructions')` — it's a thin wrapper over shared logic. Fork = clone repo, rename, customize `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md` and the adapter files.

### 3. Existing opencode integration (complete inventory)

| File                                 | Purpose                                                                      |
| ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `opencode.json`                        | Registers `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`                                     |
| `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`       | Server plugin: injects ruleset per turn, persists `/ponytail` mode switches    |
| `.opencode/plugins/entire.ts`          | **Unrelated** — Entire CLI session-tracking plugin (auto-generated)              |
| `.opencode/command/ponytail.md`        | `/ponytail <level>` slash command                                              |
| `.opencode/command/ponytail-review.md` | `/ponytail-review` slash command                                               |
| `.opencode/package.json`               | `@opencode-ai/plugin@1.17.6` dependency                                        |
| `AGENTS.md`                            | Always-on instructions (auto-loaded by OpenCode even without plugin)         |
| `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js`       | Shared instruction builder (reads `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md`, filters by mode) |
| `hooks/ponytail-config.js`             | Shared config resolver (env var → config file → default `full`)                |
| `tests/opencode-plugin.test.js`        | Smoke tests for the OpenCode plugin hooks                                    |

### 4. Ponytail vs Caveman relationship

**Complementary, not competing.** Different concerns:

|               | Ponytail                                                  | Caveman                             |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| **Governs**       | What you build (minimize code)                            | How you talk (minimize prose)       |
| **Mechanism**     | Decision ladder: YAGNI → stdlib → native → dep → one-line | Drop articles, filler, pleasantries |
| **Output**        | Shortest correct code + ≤3 skip-lines                     | Ultra-compressed communication      |
| **Token savings** | 80-94% less code                                          | ~75% less prose                     |

`skills/ponytail/SKILL.md:89` explicitly says: *"Ponytail governs what you build, not how you talk (pair with Caveman for terse prose)."*

The benchmark (`benchmarks/results/2026-06-12-caveman-vs-ponytail.md`) shows ponytail wins on code size (2.5×), caveman was initially faster until ponytail v2 added output caps. They're designed to stack.

In this session, **both are active**: caveman mode compresses my prose, ponytail mode compresses my code decisions.

### 5. Risks of creating a plugin fork

| Risk                   | Severity | Detail                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Relative path breakage** | P1       | `ponytail.mjs:17` does `require('../../hooks/ponytail-instructions')`. If the fork moves files or changes directory depth, the plugin crashes silently (the `catch` at line 82 falls back to hardcoded instructions — functional but stale). |
| **State path collision**   | P2       | Mode persisted at `~/.config/opencode/.ponytail-active` (line 22-25). Fork with different name should change this path or both plugins share one flag file — mode switches in one affect the other.                                      |
| **Dual injection**         | P2       | If both original ponytail and fork are loaded, `experimental.chat.system.transform` fires twice → double ruleset in system prompt → wasted tokens + potential instruction conflicts.                                                     |
| **`AGENTS.md` auto-load**    | P3       | OpenCode auto-loads `AGENTS.md` from repo root. If fork is checked out in the working directory AND the plugin is installed, rules inject twice (once from AGENTS.md, once from plugin).                                                 |
| **`entire.ts` plugin**       | P3       | `.opencode/plugins/entire.ts` is unrelated (session tracking). Fork should either exclude it or understand it requires Bun runtime + Entire CLI.                                                                                         |
| **Skill file drift**       | P3       | `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js:9` reads `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md` at runtime. If fork renames the skill but not the path constant, fallback instructions activate (line 82-83) — works but loses mode-specific filtering.                |
| **`check-rule-copies.js`**   | P3       | `scripts/check-rule-copies.js` validates that agent adapter files match `AGENTS.md`. Fork that changes rules must update all copies or the check fails.                                                                                    |

### What needs to change for a clean fork

1. **Rename**: `ponytail` → your name in `package.json`, `opencode.json`, plugin state path, command files
2. **State path**: Change `.ponytail-active` to `.yourname-active` in `ponytail.mjs:24`
3. **Skill path**: If renaming `skills/ponytail/`, update `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js:9`
4. **Remove `entire.ts`** unless you use Entire CLI
5. **Decide**: fork as standalone plugin (publish separately) or personal customization (just modify in place)

### Overall

**Ponytail is already a well-structured multi-agent plugin.** Forking is straightforward — it's a git fork + rename, not a restructuring. The main risk is path collisions if both original and fork are active simultaneously. The architecture is clean: shared `hooks/` + `skills/` as source of truth, thin per-agent adapters.
</task_result>
</task>

Key findings:
1. Ponytail IS already an opencode plugin (multi-agent, 10 adapters)
2. caveman-opencode-plugin is a TypeScript plugin with Bun build, 3 hooks
3. The user wants to create a fork of ponytail as a standalone opencode plugin (like caveman-opencode-plugin)

The slug should be something like "ponytail-opencode-plugin" since we're creating a standalone opencode plugin fork of ponytail.

content.summary:
## Session Intent
User requested `/create` workflow: create a fork of ponytail repo as an opencode plugin, modeled after `dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin`. Must be companion plugin pairing with `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp` for context compression. Create PRD, set up workspace, define executable tasks — ready for `/ship`.

## Key Technical Details

### Ponytail Repo Structure (already a multi-agent plugin)
- **Root**: `package.json` (v0.1.0, zero deps, pi-package descriptor), `AGENTS.md` (lazy senior dev rules), `opencode.json` (registers plugin)
- **OpenCode plugin**: `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs` — uses `experimental.chat.system.transform` + `command.execute.before` hooks
- **Shared logic**: `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js` (reads SKILL.md, filters by mode), `hooks/ponytail-config.js` (env > config.json > 'full')
- **Skills**: `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md` (canonical ruleset), `skills/ponytail-review/SKILL.md`, `skills/ponytail-help/SKILL.md`
- **Commands**: `.opencode/command/ponytail.md`, `.opencode/command/ponytail-review.md`
- **Tests**: `tests/opencode-plugin.test.js`, `tests/hooks.test.js` — Node built-in `node:test`, zero frameworks
- **Multi-agent adapters**: `.cursor/rules/`, `.windsurf/rules/`, `.clinerules/`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.kiro/steering/`, `.claude-plugin/`, `.codex-plugin/`
- **Consistency check**: `scripts/check-rule-copies.js` validates adapter copies match AGENTS.md
- **No build step** — pure JS/MJS/Markdown, no TypeScript, no bundler
- **State persistence**: `~/.config/opencode/.ponytail-active` flag file
- **Config resolution**: `PONYTAIL_DEFAULT_MODE` env → `~/.config/ponytail/config.json` → default `'full'`
- **Modes**: `off`, `lite`, `full`, `ultra`, `review`
- **`.opencode/package.json`**: depends on `@opencode-ai/plugin@1.17.6`

### caveman-opencode-plugin Reference (dantesCode)
- **Structure**: TypeScript src/ with Bun build, `dist/` output, ESM
- **Hooks**: `config` (register commands), `experimental.chat.system.transform` (inject instructions), `command.execute.before` (handle slash commands)
- **Files**: `src/index.ts` (entry), `src/config.ts` (config loader), `src/state.ts` (per-session Map), `src/commands/`, `src/skills/`, `src/__tests__/`
- **Build**: `bun run build.ts` + `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly`
- **Deps**: peerDep `@opencode-ai/plugin >=1.14.21`, zero runtime deps
- **Config**: `caveman.json` (project then global), schema: `{enabled, defaultMode, features}`
- **Published**: npm, 6 releases (v0.1.0→v0.1.5), 71 stars
- **Companion note**: "For context compression, pair with @tarquinen/opencode-dcp"

### @tarquinen/opencode-dcp (v3.1.12)
- AGPL-3.0, 3.3k stars, 10.5k weekly downloads
- Uses `experimental.chat.messages.transform` to prune obsolete tool outputs
- Custom `compress` tool registered via `tool` hook
- Runtime deps: `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer`, `@opencode-ai/sdk`, `jsonc-parser`
- Build: tsup + tsc

### OpenCode Plugin API
- Plugin = default export async function returning Hooks object
- Context: `{ project, client, $, directory, worktree }`
- Key hooks: `config`, `tool`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`, `experimental.chat.messages.transform`, `command.execute.before`, `event`, `experimental.session.compacting`
- Plugin loading: npm packages or local files, registered in `opencode.json` `plugin` array
- Plugin options: `plugin: ["name"]` or `plugin: [["name", { options }]]`

### Ponytail vs Caveman Relationship
- Complementary: ponytail governs WHAT you build (minimize code), caveman governs HOW you talk (minimize prose)
- Designed to stack together
- Both active in current session

## Artifact Map
- No files created/modified yet (research phase only)
- Files read by agents: `opencode.json`, `.opencode/plugins/ponytail.mjs`, `.opencode/package.json`, `package.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `hooks/ponytail-instructions.js`, `hooks/ponytail-config.js`, `tests/opencode-plugin.test.js`, `skills/ponytail/SKILL.md`, `scripts/check-rule-copies.js`

## Decisions
- User chose **Deep** research depth (5 agents spawned)
- Git status: on `main` branch, some untracked files (`.claude/`, `.codex/`, `.cursor/hooks.json`, `.entire/`, `.github/hooks/`, `.opencode/memory-recovery.log`, `.opencode/plugins/entire.ts`) — nothing blocking
- No active work in `.opencode/artifacts/.active`
- No memory search results (search failed)

## Problem Solving
- Identified that ponytail is already a well-structured multi-agent plugin — fork is straightforward rename + restructure for npm publishing
- Key risks identified: relative path breakage, state path collision, dual injection if both loaded, AGENTS.md auto-load duplication, entire.ts unrelated plugin

## Current State
- Research phase complete (5 agents returned results)
- Need to: initialize plan workspace, write PRD, set up branch, define tasks
- Slug will be derived from title: "create a fork of ponytail repo" → likely `ponytail-opencode-plugin`

## Continuation

- Write full PRD (complex enough for full format — multi-file, cross-cutting, npm publishing)
- Create git branch
- Define executable tasks for `/ship`

input.content:
# PRD: ponytail-opencode-plugin

## Overview

Standalone OpenCode plugin fork of [ponytail](https://github.com/user/ponytail) — the "lazy senior dev mode" behavioral ruleset. Published to npm as `ponytail-opencode-plugin`. Companion plugin to `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp` for context compression.

**Modeled after**: [dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin](https://github.com/dantesCode/caveman-opencode-plugin) — same architecture, same hook pattern, same build pipeline.

## Problem

Ponytail exists as a multi-agent plugin package (10 adapters: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Copilot, Kiro, Pi, OpenCode, generic). The OpenCode adapter is a local `.mjs` file with relative imports — not npm-publishable, not installable via `opencode plugin ponytail-opencode-plugin`.

Users who want ponytail in OpenCode must clone the repo and register a local path. This is friction.

## Solution

Extract the OpenCode adapter into a standalone npm package with:
- TypeScript source (type safety, better DX)
- Bun build pipeline (matching caveman-opencode-plugin)
- Zero runtime dependencies
- Same 3-hook pattern: `config` + `experimental.chat.system.transform` + `command.execute.before`
- Per-session state via in-memory Map (matching caveman pattern)
- Config via `ponytail.json` (project then global, matching caveman.json pattern)

## Scope

### In Scope
- TypeScript rewrite of OpenCode adapter (`src/index.ts`)
- Config loader (`src/config.ts`) — reads `ponytail.json`
- State manager (`src/state.ts`) — per-session mode tracking
- Skill/instruction generator (`src/skills/ponytail.ts`) — mode-aware instruction builder
- Command handlers (`src/commands/mode.ts`, `src/commands/review.ts`)
- Tests (`src/__tests__/`)
- Build pipeline (`build.ts` + `tsconfig.json`)
- README with install/usage docs
- npm publishing setup (`package.json`, `files`, `keywords`)

### Out of Scope
- Multi-agent adapters (Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, etc.) — this is OpenCode-only
- Pi extension — separate ecosystem
- Benchmarks, examples, docs/ — not needed for plugin
- `entire.ts` — unrelated session-tracking plugin
- `scripts/check-rule-copies.js` — no adapter copies to check
- AGENTS.md auto-load — plugin injects via hooks, not file

## Architecture

```
ponytail-opencode-plugin/
├── package.json          # npm package, peerDep @opencode-ai/plugin
├── tsconfig.json         # ES2022, ESNext module
├── build.ts              # Bun.build + tsc --emitDeclarationOnly
├── ponytail.json.example # Example config
├── README.md
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # Plugin entry — default export
│   ├── config.ts         # Config loader (ponytail.json)
│   ├── state.ts          # Per-session state (Map<sessionId, PonytailState>)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── mode.ts       # /ponytail <level> handler
│   │   └── review.ts     # /ponytail-review handler
│   ├── skills/
│   │   └── ponytail.ts   # System instruction generator (all modes)
│   └── __tests__/
│       ├── commands.test.ts
│       ├── config.test.ts
│       └── state.test.ts
└── dist/                 # Build output (published)
```

## Hook Pattern

### 1. `config` — Register commands
```typescript
config: async (opencodeConfig) => {
  opencodeConfig.command ??= {}
  opencodeConfig.command['ponytail'] = {
    template: '',
    description: 'Switch ponytail mode (off/lite/full/ultra)'
  }
  opencodeConfig.command['ponytail-review'] = {
    template: '',
    description: 'Run ponytail over-engineering review'
  }
}
```

### 2. `experimental.chat.system.transform` — Inject instructions per turn
```typescript
'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (input, output) => {
  const state = getSessionState(input.sessionID)
  if (state.mode !== 'off') {
    output.system.push(getPonytailInstructions(state.mode))
  }
}
```

### 3. `command.execute.before` — Handle slash commands
```typescript
'command.execute.before': async (input, output) => {
  if (input.command === 'ponytail') {
    const level = parseLevel(input.args)
    setSessionMode(input.sessionID, level)
    output.parts = [{ type: 'text', text: `Ponytail mode: ${level}` }]
  }
}
```

## Configuration

### `ponytail.json` (project root, then `~/.config/opencode/ponytail.json`)
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "defaultMode": "full",
  "features": {
    "ponytail": true,
    "review": true
  }
}
```

### Modes
| Mode    | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| `off`   | No instructions injected |
| `lite`  | Core ladder only |
| `full`  | Full ruleset (default) |
| `ultra` | Maximum aggression — YAGNI everything |
| `review`| Over-engineering review mode |

## Dependencies

```json
{
  "peerDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": ">=1.14.21"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@opencode-ai/plugin": "1.17.6",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "typescript": "^6.0.0"
  }
}
```

Zero runtime dependencies. Only Node stdlib (`fs`, `path`, `os`).

## Companion Plugin

README will recommend pairing with `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp`:
```jsonc
// opencode.jsonc
{
  "plugin": [
    "@tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest",
    "ponytail-opencode-plugin@latest"
  ]
}
```

DCP compresses context (input tokens). Ponytail compresses code decisions (output tokens). They stack.

## Publishing

- **npm name**: `ponytail-opencode-plugin`
- **License**: MIT (matching ponytail)
- **Keywords**: `["opencode", "plugin", "ponytail", "lazy", "senior-dev", "yagni"]`
- **Files**: `["dist"]`
- **Build**: `bun run build.ts && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly`
- **Pre-publish**: verify `dist/` exists, `package.json` correct

## Success Criteria

1. `npm publish` succeeds
2. `opencode plugin ponytail-opencode-plugin@latest` installs and activates
3. `/ponytail full` switches mode, instructions appear in system prompt
4. `/ponytail off` disables injection
5. Works alongside `@tarquinen/opencode-dcp` without conflicts
6. Works alongside `caveman-opencode-plugin` without conflicts (different state paths)
7. All tests pass (`bun test`)

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| State path collision with original ponytail | Use `ponytail-opencode-plugin` state key, not `.ponytail-active` |
| Dual injection if both original + fork loaded | README warns: use one or the other |
| Skill content drift from upstream | Pin skill content to ponytail v0.1.0 SKILL.md, document source |
| Bun runtime not available | Build output is plain ESM JS, works on Node too |

# Progress: ponytail-opencode-plugin

## Phase 1: Scaffold
- [ ] Create `package.json` (npm metadata, peerDeps, devDeps, scripts, keywords, files)
- [ ] Create `tsconfig.json` (ES2022, ESNext, bundler resolution)
- [ ] Create `build.ts` (Bun.build + tsc --emitDeclarationOnly)
- [ ] Create `ponytail.json.example`

## Phase 2: Core
- [ ] Create `src/config.ts` — config loader (project ponytail.json → global → defaults)
- [ ] Create `src/state.ts` — per-session state Map (mode, activated timestamp)
- [ ] Create `src/skills/ponytail.ts` — instruction generator (all modes, ladder, rules)
- [ ] Create `src/commands/mode.ts` — /ponytail <level> handler
- [ ] Create `src/commands/review.ts` — /ponytail-review handler
- [ ] Create `src/index.ts` — plugin entry (3 hooks: config, system.transform, command.execute.before)

## Phase 3: Tests
- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/config.test.ts`
- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/state.test.ts`
- [ ] Create `src/__tests__/commands.test.ts`
- [ ] Run `bun test` — all pass

## Phase 4: Build & Verify
- [ ] Run `bun run build.ts` — dist/ generated
- [ ] Run `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` — .d.ts files generated
- [ ] Verify dist/index.js exports default plugin function
- [ ] Smoke test: register in local opencode.json, verify hooks fire

## Phase 5: Publish Prep
- [ ] Create README.md (install, usage, config, companion plugins, modes)
- [ ] Verify `npm pack` produces correct tarball (dist/ only)
- [ ] `npm publish --dry-run` succeeds
