5: import { handleMode } from "./commands/mode";
6: import { handleReview } from "./commands/review";
7: import type { PonytailMode } from "./config";
8: 
9: const ponytailPlugin: Plugin = async () => {
10:   const hooks: Hooks = {
11:     config: async (opencodeConfig: any) => {
12:       opencodeConfig.command ??= {};
13:       opencodeConfig.command["ponytail"] = {
14:         template: "",
15:         description: "Switch ponytail mode (off/lite/full/ultra)",
16:       };
17:       opencodeConfig.command["ponytail-review"] = {
18:         template: "",
19:         description: "Run ponytail over-engineering review",
20:       };
21:     },
22: 
23:     "experimental.chat.system.transform": async (input, output) => {
24:       const cfg = loadConfig();
25:       if (!cfg.enabled || !cfg.features.ponytail) return;
26: 
27:       const sessionID = input.sessionID;
28:       if (!sessionID) return;
29: 
30:       let mode = getMode(sessionID);
31: 
32:       if (mode === "off" || !mode) {
33:         mode = cfg.defaultMode;
34:         if (mode !== "off") {
35:           setMode(sessionID, mode as PonytailMode);
36:         } else {
37:           return;
38:         }
39:       }
40: 
41:       output.system.push(getPonytailInstructions(mode as PonytailMode));
42:     },
43: 
44:     "command.execute.before": async (input, output) => {
45:       const cmd = input.command;
46:       const args = input.arguments.trim();
47:       const sessionID = input.sessionID;
48: 
49:       if (cmd === "ponytail") {
50:         const result = handleMode(sessionID, args);

52:         return;
53:       }
54:

56:         const result = handleReview(sessionID, args);
57:         output.parts = [{ type: "text", text: result.text } as any];
58:         return;
59:       }
60:     },
61:   };
62: 
63:   return hooks;
64: };
65: 
66: export default ponytailPlugin;
</content>

state.output:
<path>ponytail-opencode-plugin/src/skills/ponytail.ts</path>
<content>
1: import type { PonytailMode } from "../config";
2: 
3: const SKILL_BODY = `# Ponytail
4: 
5: You are a lazy senior developer. Lazy means efficient, not careless. You have
6: seen every over-engineered codebase and been paged at 3am for one. The best
7: code is the code never written.
8: 
9: ## Persistence
10: 
11: ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No drift back to over-building. Still active if
12: unsure. Off only: "stop ponytail" / "normal mode". Default: **full**.
13: Switch: \`/ponytail lite|full|ultra\`.
14: 
15: ## The ladder
16: 
17: Stop at the first rung that holds:
18: 
19: 1. **Does this need to exist at all?** Speculative need = skip it, say so in one line. (YAGNI)
20: 2. **Stdlib does it?** Use it.
21: 3. **Native platform feature covers it?** \`<input type="date">\` over a picker lib, CSS over JS, DB constraint over app code.
22: 4. **Already-installed dependency solves it?** Use it. Never add a new one for what a few lines can do.
23: 5. **Can it be one line?** One line.
24: 6. **Only then:** the minimum code that works.
25: 
26: The ladder is a reflex, not a research project. Two rungs work → take the
27: higher one and move on. The first lazy solution that works is the right one.
28: 
29: ## Rules
30: 
31: - No unrequested abstractions: no interface with one implementation, no factory for one product, no config for a value that never changes.
32: - No boilerplate, no scaffolding "for later", later can scaffold for itself.
33: - Deletion over addition. Boring over clever, clever is what someone decodes at 3am.
34: - Fewest files possible. Shortest working diff wins.
35: - Complex request? Ship the lazy version and question the complex request in the same response, "Did X; Y covers it. Need full X? Say so." Never stall on an answer you can default.
36: - Two stdlib options, same size? Take the one that's correct on edge cases. Lazy means writing less code, not picking the flimsier algorithm.
37: - Mark deliberate simplifications with a \`ponytail:\` comment (\`// ponytail: this exists\`), simple reads as intent, not ignorance. Shortcut with a known ceiling (global lock, O(n²) scan, naive heuristic)? The comment names the ceiling and the upgrade path: \`# ponytail: global lock, per-account locks if throughput matters\`.
38: 
39: ## Output
40: 
41: Code first. Then at most three short lines: what was skipped, when to add it.
42: No essays, no feature tours, no design notes. If the explanation is longer
43: than the code, delete the explanation, every paragraph defending a
44: simplification is complexity smuggled back in as prose.
45: 
46: Pattern: \`[code] → skipped: [X], add when [Y].\`
47: 
48: ## Intensity
49: 
50: | Level | What change                                                                                                                 |
51: | ----- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
52: | **lite**  | Build what's asked, but name the lazier alternative in one line. User picks.                                                |
53: | **full**  | The ladder enforced. Stdlib and native first. Shortest diff, shortest explanation. Default.                                 |
54: | **ultra** | YAGNI extremist. Deletion before addition. Ship the one-liner and challenge the rest of the requirement in the same breath. |
55: 
56: Example: "Add a cache for these API responses."
57: - lite: "Done, cache added. FYI: \`functools.lru_cache\` covers this in one line if you'd rather not own a cache class."
58: - full: "\`@lru_cache(maxsize=1000)\` on the fetch function. Skipped custom cache class, add when lru_cache measurably falls short."
59: - ultra: "No cache until a profiler says so. When it does: \`@lru_cache\`. A hand-rolled TTL cache class is a bug farm with a hit rate."
60: 
61: ## When NOT to be lazy
62: 
63: Never simplify away: input validation at trust boundaries, error handling
64: that prevents data loss, security measures, accessibility basics, anything
65: explicitly requested. User insists on the full version → build it, no
66: re-arguing.
67: 
68: Non-trivial logic (a branch, a loop, a parser, a money/security path) leaves
69: ONE runnable check behind, the smallest thing that fails if the logic
70: breaks: an \`assert\`-based \`demo()\`/\`__main__\` self-check or one small
71: \`test_*.py\`. No frameworks, no fixtures, no per-function suites unless
72: asked. Trivial one-liners need no test, YAGNI applies to tests too.
73: 
74: ## Boundaries
75: 
76: Ponytail governs what you build, not how you talk (pair with Caveman for
77: terse prose). "stop ponytail" / "normal mode": revert. Level persists until
78: changed or session end.
79: 
80: The shortest path to done is the right path.`;
81: 
82: function filterForMode(body: string, mode: PonytailMode): string {
83:   return body
84:     .split(/\r?\n/)
85:     .filter((line) => {
86:       // Filter intensity table rows: | **mode** | ... |
87:       const tableMatch = line.match(/^\|\s*\*\*(.+?)\*\*\s*\|/);
88:       if (tableMatch) {
89:         const labelMode = tableMatch[1].trim().toLowerCase();
90:         if (["lite", "full", "ultra"].includes(labelMode)) {
91:           return labelMode === mode;
92:         }
93:       }
94: 
95:       // Filter example bullets: - mode: ...
96:       const exampleMatch = line.match(/^-\s*([^:]+):\s*/);
97:       if (exampleMatch) {
98:         const labelMode = exampleMatch[1].trim().toLowerCase();
99:         if (["lite", "full", "ultra"].includes(labelMode)) {
100:           return labelMode === mode;
101:         }
102:       }
103: 
104:       return true;
105:     })
106:     .join("\n");
107: }
108: 
109: export function getPonytailInstructions(mode: PonytailMode): string {
110:   if (mode === "review") {
111:     return `PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE — level: review. Behavior defined by /ponytail-review skill.`;
112:   }
113: 
114:   return `PONYTAIL MODE ACTIVE — level: ${mode}\n\n${filterForMode(SKILL_BODY, mode)}`;
115: }
116: 
117: export function getReviewInstructions(): string {
118:   return `You review code for over-engineering, ponytail style.
119: Rules:
120: - One line per finding.
121: - Format: L<line>: <severity> <problem>. <fix>.
122: - Severities: yagni (unnecessary), bloat (over-engineered), nit (style), q (question).
123: - Focus on: unused abstractions, speculative features, avoidable dependencies, boilerplate.
124: - Drop throat-clearing. Keep exact line numbers and concrete fixes.`;
125: }
</content>

1: import { readFileSync, existsSync } from "fs";
2: import { join } from "path";
3: import { homedir } from "os";
4: import { cwd } from "process";
5: 
6: export type PonytailMode = "off" | "lite" | "full" | "ultra" | "review";
7: 
8: export interface PonytailConfig {
9:   enabled: boolean;
10:   defaultMode: PonytailMode;
11:   features: {
12:     ponytail: boolean;
13:     review: boolean;
14:   };
15: }
16: 
17: const defaults: PonytailConfig = {
18:   enabled: true,
19:   defaultMode: "full",
20:   features: {
21:     ponytail: true,
22:     review: true,
23:   },
24: };
25: 
26: function loadConfigFile(filePath: string): Partial<PonytailConfig> | null {
27:   if (!existsSync(filePath)) return null;
28:   try {
29:     return JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8"));
30:   } catch {
31:     return null;
32:   }
33: }
34: 
35: export function loadConfig(): PonytailConfig {
36:   const paths = [
37:     join(cwd(), "ponytail.json"),
38:     join(homedir(), ".config", "opencode", "ponytail.json"),
39:   ];
40: 
41:   for (const p of paths) {
42:     const cfg = loadConfigFile(p);
43:     if (cfg) {
44:       return {
45:         ...defaults,
46:         ...cfg,
47:         features: { ...defaults.features, ...cfg.features },
48:       };
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   return defaults;
53: }
54: 
55: export const VALID_MODES: PonytailMode[] = ["off", "lite", "full", "ultra", "review"];
56: export const RUNTIME_MODES: PonytailMode[] = ["off", "lite", "full", "ultra"];
57: 
58: export function isValidMode(mode: string): mode is PonytailMode {
59:   return VALID_MODES.includes(mode as PonytailMode);
60: }
61: 
62: export function isRuntimeMode(mode: string): mode is PonytailMode {
63:   return RUNTIME_MODES.includes(mode as PonytailMode);
64: }
</content>
